exemplar.title: Can new Knowledge change established values and beliefs?

detailedExemplarData.title: Can new Knowledge change established values and beliefs?

strengths: Three well chosen, authentic real-world objects clearly identified with specific contexts.
Explicit, coherent links to the IA prompt for each object, showing how new knowledge can shift values and beliefs.
Robust use of evidence (historical data on DDT bans, quotations from political sources, scriptural verses) to support claims.
Clear framing of the exhibition around the chosen prompt and consistent focus on TOK concepts.
Good academic practice demonstrated through a comprehensive references section.
weaknesses: Depth and precision of analysis unevenâespecially in the third object, which remains overly descriptive and personal.
Various grammatical lapses, ambiguous phrasing and a few typographical errors (e.g. âDTTâ instead of âDDTâ).
Incomplete citations and formatting inconsistencies (missing dates on images, conflated URLs, lack of chapter/verse for Scripture).
Some assertions lack in-text evidence or clear linkage to TOK theory, reducing overall analytical rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs exhibition meets the descriptors of the 7â8 band by presenting three distinct, real objects with fully stated contexts and by making clear, well-justified links to the prompt âCan new knowledge change established values and beliefs?â. Most points are supported by concrete evidence and appropriate references. However, uneven depthâparticularly in the analysis of the third objectâalong with occasional grammatical lapses, missing citations and formatting inconsistencies prevent entry into the top band. On balance, the work demonstrates solid understanding and application of TOK concepts with only minor weaknesses, justifying a final score of 8.

annotations.comment: The exhibition clearly states the chosen IA prompt, ensuring focus on âCan new knowledge change established values and beliefs?â throughout. Good framing of the task.
The student identifies a specific edition and contextualizes the first object effectively, linking its publication history to its significance for the prompt.
Consider using a photograph of your actual first edition of Silent Spring rather than a generic stock image to strengthen authenticity and context.
There is a typographical error in the pesticide acronym (DTT instead of DDT). Ensure precise use of terminology for scientific credibility.
The link to the IA prompt is present but could be more explicit: directly discuss how Carsonâs data challenged existing societal beliefs rather than general fascination.
The student provides strong evidence of DDTâs ban and societal shift, supporting the objectâs role in illustrating knowledge-driven value change with concrete historical data.
Format the âObject 2â header consistently (e.g., use heading tags) to improve visual structure and clarity between objects.
The absence of a caption detailing the date and source of the photo of Almameh Gibbaâs case limits the context; include full citation and date for clarity.
The student effectively situates the second object within a Global Politics lesson, demonstrating real-world context and relevance to FGM debates.
Break long sentences into shorter ones and add inline citations for claims about the UN and clerics to improve readability and academic rigor.
The concluding sentence of object 2 is confusing (âinheritance continuedâ); clarify the thought to fully articulate how cultural beliefs persist despite evidence.
The third object is clearly introduced with context, indicating the personal connection and relevance to the IA prompt.
The commentary on the Quran is heavily personal; deepen the analysis by explaining specifically how its teachings interact with TOK concepts of values and knowledge.
The exposition on the Quran repeats points about kindness; streamline to avoid redundancy and focus on deeper analysis.
Provide precise quotations and citations for Quranic and Biblical verses (e.g., chapter and verse numbers) and discuss their epistemic implications.
The references section demonstrates good academic practice in acknowledging sources at the end of the exhibition.
The Aljazeera citation and URL are conflated; ensure each source entry is on its own line and follows consistent formatting (APA or MLA).
Include retrieval dates for online sources uniformly and confirm that all URLs are hyperlinked or formatted correctly for accessibility.
Expand the abbreviation âE.S.S.â upon first use and explain its relevance to TOK to avoid confusion and enhance context.
Link Carsonâs ecocentric values to TOK concepts (such as perspective and paradigm shifts) to deepen the theoretical analysis.
Deepen the link to TOK by referencing knowledge communities and the authority of expertise when discussing resistance to FGM evidence.
Specify the provenance of the roommateâs Qurâan (e.g., edition, translation) to strengthen its role as an authentic object in the exhibition.
There is no concluding paragraph synthesizing insights across all three objects; consider adding a reflection to tie together themes and TOK implications.
Revise the phrase âThis exhibition particularly interests this objectâ to âThis object is particularly interestingâ for clarity and fluency.
Incorrect word choice (âminoring many Christian teachingsâ vs. âmirroringâ); revise for accuracy.
Consider engaging with counterclaims or non-religious perspectives on universal values to deepen the TOK discussion on morality.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
